Document:
# CIVIL CITATIONS Minor legal alternatives to full arrest. May be issued by Marshals, Colonial Administration, or authorized department heads.
Types include: warnings, fines, restitution, temporary confiscation, access or license suspension, community service, or short holding. May not replace arrest for major or capital crimes. If refused, the offender serves the listed holding sentence instead.
# ARRESTING PROCEDURES [bold]Standard arrest:[/bold] Inform the suspect of the charge, order compliance, use non-lethal force unless a lethal threat is present, restrain, move to holding, process charges promptly. Lethal force is only authorized if the suspect poses an immediate lethal threat to officers, civilians, or critical colony systems.
[bold]Civilian detainment:[/bold] Any civilian may detain another only if they witness a major or capital crime and Law Enforcement is unavailable. Must be handed to Law Enforcement as soon as practical. May not be used for minor offenses.
[bold]Field detainment:[/bold] During emergencies or off-colony operations, authorized personnel may detain persons who endanger the group, interfere with evacuation, violate quarantine, sabotage equipment, or interfere with emergency operations. Must be transferred to proper authority as soon as practical.
